steps.text: Verify the gas dilution system per Section 3.2 of Method 205 using any available protocol gas and corresponding reference method.

preconditions.text: Daily or per use (whichever is less stringent)

steps.text: Assemble an evacuated canister with the sampling device including filter connected to a certified flow meter.
If the unit cannot be cleaned to meet the specifications, retire the unit from use or repurpose for source sampling.
Collect a humidified (> 40% RH) known-standard challenge gas through the sampling device and into a canister.
Analyze the sample according to Section 11 of this method.
Record the concentration measurement and maintain the results as part of a laboratory record associated with the sampling device.
For compounds exceeding the ± 15% criterion, take steps to eliminate the bias (e.g., cleaning).

preconditions.text: Conduct the sampling device standard check prior to initial use, at least every six months, and after replacement of components, or collection of potentially contaminating samples.
Use only canisters that have been qualified as specified in Section 8.3 of this method.

constraints.text: The challenge gas must contain the target oHAPs at 100 to 500 pptv each, chosen considering the expected measured concentration at the deployment location(s).
The results must demonstrate that each oHAP in the sample collected through the sampling device is within ± 15% of the concentrations in the reference sample.

steps.text: d canister with a humidified (40 to 50% RH) standard gas in HCF zero air with each target compound at approximately 100 to 500 pptv.
Choose the selected challenge concentration based on the concentration expected to be measured during the sampling event.

Attach the canisters to an oil-free roughing pump and evacuate to approximately 7 kPa absolute (28 in. Hg vacuum or 1.0 psia) with the exhaust of the pump directed to a fume hood or passed through a charcoal trap.
Refill canisters to ambient pressure with HCF zero air.
Attach the canisters to the cleaning system after completing the pre-evacuation and refilling steps.

constraints.text: Potential for contamination of the system

steps.text: Place the canister in a location that protects the canister and sampling inlet from unwanted tampering, damage, or theft.
Protect the canister and sampling inlets by placing the canister under shelter, if possible.
Determine the appropriate number and placement of sampling locations at the fenceline based on the applicable standard.
Document all activities associated with sampling on the field data page.
Verify that each canister has been cleaned and blanked within the last 30 days.
Label each canister with a sample ID code and record the canister and sample ID on the field data page.

preconditions.text: The canister must have been cleaned and blanked within 30 days of sampling.

constraints.text: Horizontal distances should be > 10 meters (m) from biasing sources (e.g., outdoor smoking areas, vehicle exhaust, HVAC units, building exhaust, outdoor fuel storage, shelter roofing, or other sample collection exhaust).
Do not restrict air flow around inlets and do not locate inlets under building overhangs.
Do not place the canister near vegetation or structures that block or significantly restrict air flow to the MFCD inlet or manifold.
Ensure that rain cannot be drawn directly into the MFCD, and the inlet heights must be approximately 1.5 to 3 m above ground level.
All sampling locations must initiate sampling within 60 minutes of each other.

steps.text: Label each canister with a sample ID code and record the canister and sample ID on the field data page.
Verify the sample device is in working order and calibrate/verify the flow rate setting, if applicable, with a reference flow meter.
Record the sample device ID, expected flowrate, and the reference flowrate if calibrated/verified in the field, including the reference flow meter if applicable.
Attach the sampling device to the canister and locate at the appropriate sampling location.
Record the sampling location ID, latitude, longitude, date, and time that you installed the canister on the field data page.
Measure and record the canister vacuum using the field pressure measurement gauge, and verify that the canister has not leaked and has sufficient vacuum to collect the sample.

preconditions.text: The canister must be cleaned and blanked within 30 days of sampling.

steps.text: Collect canister field blanks for each sample day.
Prepare a canister blank by filling a canister with humidified clean diluent gas to approximately -15 in. Hg ± 1 in. Hg.
Record the pressure of the canister blank.
Transport the canister blank to the field site(s) to accompany field-collected canisters.
Analyze the field blanks by interspersing them among the field samples.
Collect a canister field spike for each sample day.

Prepare a canister field spike by filling a canister with humidified standard gas at a concentration in the lower third of the calibration curve for the target compound to approximately -15 in. Hg ± 1 in. Hg.
Transport the field spike canister to the field site to accompany field-collected canisters.
Analyze the field spikes.

constraints.text: Canister field blanks are to be treated identically to field-collected samples in the field and laboratory including pressure checks, MFCD leak checks, etc.
The field spike canister is treated identically to field-collected samples in the field and laboratory, including pressure checks, MFCD leak checks, etc.

steps.text: Open the canister valves.
Document the leak check results in the analysis records.
Connect each sample canister to the preconcentration unit through a port.
Verify each canister as having a leak-free connection.
Accurately measure the sample aliquot volume for analysis by metering the sample with an MFC or with the combination of a fixed-volume vessel and a pressure transducer.
Perform the steps for readying the system and performing the GC-MS analytical sequence.
Verify the instrument calibration by analysis of a CCV.
Begin sample analysis.

preconditions.text: Prior to each sample analysis sequence
Once these checks meet criteria (summarized in Table 9-1 of this method)

constraints.text: Sample introduction volume measurements must be made by the same device as the calibration standards to ensure that analyzed volumes of samples and standards are consistent.

steps.text: Identify target compounds qualitatively based on their RT and the relative abundance of their characteristic ions from the MS.

preconditions.text: The RT of the compound must be within the RT window of ± 2 seconds of the most recent calibration check.
The relative abundance ratio of qualifier ion response to target ion response for at least one qualifier ion must be within ± 30% of the average relative abundance ratio from the ICAL.
The S:N ratio of the target and qualifier ions must be > 3:1.
If any of the four criteria are not met, the compound cannot be positively identified.

steps.text: Pre-evacuate the canister.
Perform a minimum of 5 evacuation cycles with a purge gas temperature of 80 °C and 50% humidity.
Normalize all ion abundances to m/z 95.

preconditions.text: Depending on the canister type, higher purge gas temperatures may be required.

constraints.text: Do not exceed the manufacturer’s recommended maximum temperatures for component parts such as valves and gauges.
